creator: DBRICESHULTZ@GMAIL.COM
text:
During  February when  my Gigabyte 10 Year Old Computer failed,  I purchased

   a  Gigabyte  7370XP SLI  Board  and a new Intel Processor.  I  then  added

   16 GB of Memory and  loaded  FreeBSD 11.1  on  the rebuilt  Computer.

   I  installed  XORG  XFCE4 Libreoffice  and  others  I  installed  

   Firefox.  The  internet  numbers  worked  well.  I  failed  somehow  to

   enable  BIND  to  work  properly.  Therefore  I  executed  dhclient and  
   the     "BIND"  functioned  and   I  installed   "Firefox"  which  worked
   normally  until  I  responded  to  the  message  "This version of Firefox 
   Cannot  be  Supported".  I  responded  to the complaint of "Mozilla Firefox"

   The  fabulous  FreeBSD  "pkg"  ( install  info  update upgrade etc ),         
   performed  and  I  was  messaged  that  "Firefox"  had been improved.

   Currently  Firefox  cannot  find  freebsd.org  or FreeBSD.org or  
   Wikipedia.org or  FSF.org or fsf.org  or scientificlinux.org or  any.org.

   FreeBSD 11.1  will  ping  the  IP numbers of FreeBSD.org  i.e 96.47.72.84
   rapidly  but  will  not  ping  FreeBSD.org,  as  will the  current  10.2
   FreeBSD  installation.  I  do  not  know  if  the  GIGABYTE  7370XP SLI is
   creating  the  problem  possibly,   

   Sadly  I  previously  had  more  time to  work with FreeBSD and I had studied
   "C"  and Postscript and CISCO  OS.  Perhaps  being  busy  I  have  lost some
   knowledge,  I  am  also  requesting  more  contact  with  the  system.


    Thank  all  of   you  for  your  fabulous  creations  and  contributions.
